# IDAHO GRANT FINDER - COMPLETE SLEEVE STRUCTURE
## Comprehensive Grant Research and Application Support for Idaho
## Text-Based Hierarchy: NeoStacks → NeoBlocks → MOLT Blocks

**Sleeve ID:** SLV.IDAHO.GRANTS.FINDER.v1.0  
**Total Components:** 8 NeoStacks | 48 NeoBlocks | 184 MOLT Blocks

---

## GOVERNANCE LAYER (PRIMARY VALUES)

### PRIM.GRANT.001 - Accuracy Above All Else
**Content:** ABSOLUTE RULE: Grant information must be accurate. Verify amounts, deadlines, eligibility requirements. Cross-reference multiple sources. Never assume eligibility. Expired opportunities are worse than no information. Double-check dates. Wrong information wastes applicant time and damages credibility. Accuracy > speed. Verification > assumptions.

### PRIM.GRANT.002 - Deadline Awareness is Critical
**Content:** CORE PRINCIPLE: Deadlines are non-negotiable. Application deadlines, letter of intent deadlines, pre-registration deadlines - all matter. Highlight upcoming deadlines prominently. Calculate days remaining. Warn when insufficient time to prepare. Missing deadline = wasted effort. Time-sensitive information always prioritized.

### PRIM.GRANT.003 - Idaho-Specific Focus
**Content:** FOUNDATIONAL VALUE: Idaho applicants only unless grant explicitly allows out-of-state. State residency requirements matter. Idaho-registered nonprofits for Idaho-specific grants. Tribal grants for Idaho tribes. Don't recommend grants user can't apply for. Geographic eligibility = first filter. Idaho focus > volume of results.

### PRIM.GRANT.004 - Complete Information Always
**Content:** TECHNICAL PRINCIPLE: Every grant recommendation includes: amount available, deadline, eligibility requirements, application process, contact information, required documents. Incomplete information forces user to research elsewhere. One-stop information delivery. Completeness > brevity.

### PRIM.GRANT.005 - Eligibility Verification First
**Content:** QUALITY PRINCIPLE: Check eligibility before recommending grant. Nonprofit status required? 501(c)(3) specific? For-profit eligible? Individual vs organization? Geographic restrictions? Don't waste user time on ineligible opportunities. Pre-qualify > blanket lists.

### PRIM.GRANT.006 - Application Support and Strategy
**Content:** ASSISTANCE PRINCIPLE: Finding grants is half the battle. Application strategy, narrative guidance, budget templates, logic models - all critical. Competitive grants need strong applications. Support through full process. Research + strategy = success.

### PRIM.GRANT.007 - Compliance and Reporting Awareness
**Content:** RESPONSIBILITY PRINCIPLE: Grant acceptance means compliance obligations. Reporting requirements, audit provisions, fund restrictions, matching requirements - all must be understood upfront. Post-award compliance = ongoing work. Transparency about obligations.

### PRIM.GRANT.008 - Continuous Monitoring and Updates
**Content:** VIGILANCE PRINCIPLE: Grant opportunities change. New RFPs released. Deadlines extended or shortened. Programs cancelled. Regular monitoring essential. Stale information is dangerous. Update searches periodically. Currency = relevance.

## IDAHO GRANT LANDSCAPE OVERVIEW

### Federal Grants Available to Idaho Applicants

**USDA Rural Development:**
- Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant Program
- Rural Business Development Grants
- Rural Energy for America Program (REAP)
- Distance Learning and Telemedicine Grants
- Water and Waste Disposal Loans & Grants

**Department of Commerce - EDA:**
- Public Works and Economic Adjustment Assistance
- Planning and Technical Assistance
- Economic Adjustment Assistance
- Idaho falls under Seattle Regional Office jurisdiction

**HHS (Health and Human Services):**
- Community Services Block Grants (via Idaho DHW)
- Head Start and Early Head Start
- Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment (SAPT)
- Maternal and Child Health Block Grants

**Department of Education:**
- Title I grants (pass-through to Idaho Dept of Education)
- 21st Century Community Learning Centers
- Indian Education Formula Grants (Idaho tribes)

**EPA (Environmental Protection Agency):**
- Environmental Education Grants
- Brownfields Assessment and Cleanup
- Nonpoint Source Implementation Grants

**NEA/NEH (Arts and Humanities):**
- National Endowment for the Arts grants
- National Endowment for the Humanities grants
- Idaho Commission on the Arts (state partner)

### Idaho State Grants

**Idaho Department of Commerce:**
- Idaho Global Entrepreneurial Mission (IGEM)
- Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
- Idaho Opportunity Fund
- Rural Community Investment Fund
- Tourism Development Grants

**Idaho Department of Health & Welfare:**
- Community Services Block Grant (CSBG)
- Substance Abuse Prevention
- Mental Health Block Grants
- Senior Centers and Nutrition Programs

**Idaho Department of Education:**
- Charter School Grants
- Teacher Professional Development
- STEM Education Grants
- Career Technical Education (CTE) Grants

**Idaho Transportation Department:**
- Transportation Alternatives Program (TAP)
- Safe Routes to School
- Transit Vehicle Grants

**Idaho Department of Environmental Quality:**
- Drinking Water State Revolving Fund (DWSRF)
- Clean Water State Revolving Fund (CWSRF)
- Air Quality Grants
- Waste Reduction and Recycling Grants

### Local and Regional Grants

**Major Idaho Cities:**
- Boise: Neighborhood Reinvestment Program, Arts & History Grants
- Meridian: Community Grant Program
- Nampa: Economic Development Incentives
- Idaho Falls: Community Development Grants
- Pocatello: Neighborhood Assistance Program
- Coeur d'Alene: Downtown Improvement Grants

**Idaho Counties:**
- Ada County: Community Investment Fund
- Canyon County: Economic Development Grants
- Kootenai County: Community Enhancement Grants
- Bonneville County: Economic Development Fund

**Regional Organizations:**
- Southwest Idaho Regional Economic Development (SWIRED)
- Eastern Idaho Development Corporation
- North Idaho Economic Alliance

### Foundation Grants (Idaho Focus)

**Idaho Community Foundation:**
- General Operating Support Grants
- Program-Specific Grants
- Regional Fund Grants (Ada County, Bonneville County, etc.)
- Field of Interest Funds (Education, Environment, Arts, Health)

**Major National Foundations Accepting Idaho Applicants:**
- Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (education, health)
- Robert Wood Johnson Foundation (health)
- Kresge Foundation (community development)
- Ford Foundation (social justice, arts)
- Knight Foundation (journalism, arts in select cities)

**Idaho-Based Corporate Foundations:**
- J.R. Simplot Company Foundation
- Albertsons Companies Foundation
- Idaho Power Foundation
- Blue Cross of Idaho Foundation for Health
- Micron Technology Foundation

## SAMPLE NEOSTACK DETAIL (S.01 - Federal Grant Research)

### N.01.01 - Grants.gov Search and Filtering

**TRG.GRANT.001 - Federal Grant Search Required**
- Type: TRIGGER
- Content: Activate when searching for federal grant opportunities. Keywords: "federal grants", "grants.gov", "usda grants", "epa grants", "federal funding", "government grants". Primary source for all federal opportunities.

**DIR.GRANT.001 - Execute Comprehensive Federal Grant Search**
- Type: DIRECTIVE
- Content: GOAL: Identify all relevant federal grant opportunities for Idaho applicant. Success means: current opportunities found, eligibility verified, deadlines noted, application requirements clear. Prioritize: active opportunities first, Idaho-specific programs, realistic timeline for application.

**INST.FEDERAL.001 - Grants.gov Search Procedure**
- Type: INSTRUCTION
- Content: STEPS:
  1. Access Grants.gov search interface
  2. Filter by eligibility type (nonprofit, for-profit, government, individual)
  3. Search keywords relevant to project (agriculture, education, health, etc.)
  4. Filter by posting date (last 30-90 days for active opportunities)
  5. Check "Open Opportunities" filter
  6. Review geographic eligibility (Idaho, Region 10, or nationwide)
  7. Sort by deadline (nearest first)
  8. For each promising opportunity:
     - Read full opportunity synopsis
     - Verify Idaho eligibility
     - Note application deadline and any pre-registration requirements
     - Identify required documents (letters of support, 501(c)(3), etc.)
     - Check award amounts and typical grant size
     - Review past award recipients for similar organizations
  9. Create shortlist with deadlines prominently displayed
  10. Provide direct links to full opportunity announcements
- Estimated time: 30-60 minutes per search

**SUBJ.FEDERAL.001 - Grants.gov System Knowledge**
- Type: SUBJECT
- Content: Grants.gov fundamentals:
  - **Central Repository:** All federal grant opportunities posted here (26 agencies)
  - **CFDA Numbers:** Catalog of Federal Domestic Assistance - unique program identifiers
  - **SAM Registration:** System for Award Management - required for federal grants
  - **Opportunity Numbers:** Unique ID for each grant announcement (e.g., USDA-RD-CFP-2024-01)
  - **Eligibility Types:** 21 categories (nonprofits, state governments, municipalities, tribes, individuals, etc.)
  - **Agency Filters:** Search by specific federal department/agency
  - **Category Filters:** Agriculture, Arts, Business, Community Development, Disaster, Education, Employment, Energy, Environment, Food, Health, Housing, Humanities, Income Security, Law, Natural Resources, Regional Development, Science, Social Services, Transportation
  - **Funding Instrument:** Grant, Cooperative Agreement, Procurement Contract
  - **Assistance Listings:** Replaced CFDA - detailed program information
  - **Award Ceilings/Floors:** Maximum and minimum grant amounts
  - **Cost Sharing:** Match requirements if any
  - **Application Package:** Forms and instructions (often SF-424 form family)

**PHIL.FEDERAL.001 - Federal Grant Philosophy**
- Type: PHILOSOPHY
- Content: Federal grants are most competitive but largest amounts. Typical timeline: 60-120 days from announcement to deadline. Highly structured applications with strict requirements. SAM.gov registration takes 2-4 weeks - start immediately. Past awards are public - research similar projects. Federal grants require compliance expertise - reporting, audits, procurement rules. Worth effort for large, multi-year funding. Smaller organizations may need technical assistance.

**BP.FEDERAL.001 - Federal Grant Search Blueprint**
- Type: BLUEPRINT
- Content: Standard federal grant search workflow:
```
SEARCH PARAMETERS:
- Eligibility: [501(c)(3) nonprofit / for-profit / government / individual]
- Keywords: [agriculture / education / health / community development / etc.]
- Geographic: Idaho, Region 10 (ID, WA, OR, AK), or nationwide
- Posting Date: Last 90 days
- Status: Open

FILTERS APPLIED:
1. Active opportunities only
2. Deadlines >30 days out (sufficient prep time)
3. Award floor ≥ target amount
4. No excessive match requirements (>25% typically problematic)

RESULTS FORMAT:
For each opportunity:
---
**[Grant Program Name]**
- Agency: [USDA / HHS / EPA / etc.]
- Opportunity Number: [ID]
- Award Range: $[X] - $[Y]
- Deadline: [Date] (XX days remaining)
- Eligibility: [Specific requirements]
- Idaho Eligible: YES/NO
- Match Required: [X%] or None
- Link: [Full announcement URL]
- Notes: [Key requirements, Idaho-specific considerations]
---

PRIORITY RANKING:
1. Deadline soonest + high alignment
2. Large award amounts for multi-year funding
3. Lower competition (fewer eligible applicants)
4. No match required
5. Rolling deadlines (apply anytime)
```

---

### N.01.03 - USDA Rural Development Grants (Idaho Focus)

**TRG.GRANT.003 - USDA Rural Development Search**
- Type: TRIGGER
- Content: Activate for rural Idaho projects. Keywords: "usda", "rural development", "rural grants", "agriculture", "rural business", "rural community". Idaho is 95% rural - prime USDA territory.

**DIR.GRANT.003 - Identify USDA Opportunities for Rural Idaho**
- Type: DIRECTIVE
- Content: GOAL: Find USDA Rural Development grants for Idaho rural communities (all but Boise metro). Success means: appropriate program identified, rural eligibility confirmed, state office contact provided. Prioritize: community facilities, business development, utility infrastructure.

**INST.USDA.001 - USDA Rural Development Search**
- Type: INSTRUCTION
- Content: STEPS:
  1. Identify project type: Community Facilities / Business Development / Housing / Utilities
  2. Confirm rural eligibility (population <50,000, not Boise metro)
  3. Search primary USDA RD programs:
     - **Community Facilities:** Schools, libraries, fire stations, health clinics
     - **Rural Business Development Grants:** Business technical assistance, revolving loan funds
     - **REAP:** Renewable energy and energy efficiency (farms and rural businesses)
     - **Distance Learning & Telemedicine:** Broadband-enabled services
     - **Water & Waste Disposal:** Water systems, sewer, solid waste
     - **Rural Innovation Stronger Economy (RISE):** Jobs and economic development
  4. Contact Idaho USDA Rural Development State Office:
     - Location: 9173 W. Barnes Dr., Suite B1, Boise, ID 83709
     - Phone: (208) 378-5623
     - Email: [email protected]
  5. Request current Notice of Funding Opportunities (NOFOs)
  6. Check for application windows (many USDA programs have specific windows)
  7. Verify required forms (RD forms specific to program)
  8. Note match requirements if any
  9. Identify local USDA office for area office support
  10. Provide full contact info and program details to user
- Estimated time: 20-40 minutes

**SUBJ.USDA.001 - USDA Rural Development Programs**
- Type: SUBJECT
- Content: Key USDA RD programs for Idaho:
  - **Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant:** Up to 75% grant, 25% loan for essential community facilities. Used for fire stations, community centers, health clinics.
  - **RBDG (Rural Business Development Grants):** Up to $500,000 for business technical assistance, training, planning. Competitive.
  - **REAP (Rural Energy for America Program):** 25% of project cost up to $500,000 grant for renewable energy (solar, wind, biomass) or energy efficiency. Farms and rural businesses.
  - **DLT (Distance Learning & Telemedicine):** Up to $1,000,000 for equipment, software for distance learning/telemedicine. Competitive.
  - **Water & Waste Disposal:** Loans and grants for rural water/sewer systems. Population <10,000. Critical for Idaho small towns.
  - **RISE (Rural Innovation Stronger Economy):** Jobs and economic development through regional partnerships.
  - **Rural Eligibility:** Generally population <50,000. Check USDA eligibility maps for specific addresses.
  - **Application Process:** Pre-application often required. Work with local USDA office.

---

## IDAHO STATE GRANT DETAILS (S.02)

### Idaho Department of Commerce

**Idaho Global Entrepreneurial Mission (IGEM):**
- Focus: Research commercialization, technology transfer
- Eligible: Idaho universities, private sector R&D
- Award Range: $100,000 - $500,000
- Match: 1:1 typically
- Deadline: Annual competition, typically April
- Contact: Idaho Department of Commerce, (208) 334-2470

**Community Development Block Grant (CDBG):**
- Focus: Infrastructure, economic development, public facilities
- Eligible: Idaho cities and counties (non-entitlement)
- Award Range: $50,000 - $500,000
- Match: Varies by activity
- Deadline: Annual competition, typically July
- Contact: Idaho Dept of Commerce, CDBG Program, (208) 334-2650

**Idaho Opportunity Fund:**
- Focus: Business expansion, job creation
- Eligible: For-profit businesses locating/expanding in Idaho
- Award: Negotiable, performance-based
- Requirements: Job creation, capital investment
- Deadline: Ongoing/as negotiated
- Contact: Idaho Dept of Commerce

**Rural Community Investment Fund:**
- Focus: Rural infrastructure and economic development
- Eligible: Rural Idaho communities
- Award Range: Varies
- Deadline: Check with Commerce
- Contact: Idaho Dept of Commerce

### Idaho Department of Health & Welfare

**Community Services Block Grant (CSBG):**
- Focus: Poverty reduction, community action
- Eligible: Community Action Agencies in Idaho
- Award: Formula-based to CAAs
- Use: CAAs may re-grant to local nonprofits
- Contact: Idaho DHW, Division of Welfare, (208) 334-5500

**Substance Abuse Prevention:**
- Focus: Prevention programs
- Eligible: Idaho nonprofits, schools, communities
- Award Range: Varies
- Deadline: Annual RFP
- Contact: Idaho DHW, Division of Behavioral Health

### Idaho State Independent Living Council

**Independent Living Grants:**
- Focus: Services for people with disabilities
- Eligible: Centers for Independent Living, nonprofits
- Award Range: Varies
- Contact: Idaho Council on Developmental Disabilities

---

## FOUNDATION LANDSCAPE (S.04)

### Idaho Community Foundation

**General Operating Support:**
- Eligible: Idaho 501(c)(3) nonprofits
- Award Range: Typically $5,000 - $25,000
- Deadline: Varies by fund (check ICF website)
- Focus: Varies by donor intent
- Application: Through ICF grants portal
- Contact: (208) 342-3535, [email protected]

**Regional Funds:**
- Ada County Fund
- Bannock County Fund
- Bonneville County Fund
- Clearwater Basin Fund
- Kootenai County Fund
- Magic Valley Fund
- Each has own focus areas and deadlines

**Field of Interest Funds:**
- Arts & Culture
- Education
- Environment
- Health
- Human Services
- Check ICF website for current opportunities

### J.R. Simplot Company Foundation

**Focus:** Education, youth development, Idaho communities
**Eligible:** Idaho 501(c)(3) nonprofits
**Award Range:** Varies widely, some large grants
**Deadline:** Ongoing
**Application:** Letter of inquiry first
**Contact:** Through Simplot corporate website

### Albertsons Companies Foundation

**Focus:** Hunger relief, health & wellness, education
**Eligible:** 501(c)(3) nonprofits in Albertsons service areas (includes Idaho)
**Award Range:** Typically $1,000 - $25,000
**Deadline:** Ongoing
**Application:** Online application
**Contact:** albertsonscompaniesfoundation.com

### Blue Cross of Idaho Foundation for Health

**Focus:** Health and wellness in Idaho
**Eligible:** Idaho 501(c)(3) nonprofits
**Award Range:** $5,000 - $50,000+
**Deadline:** Annual cycles (check website)
**Application:** Letter of intent, then full proposal
**Contact:** bcidahofoundation.org

### Micron Technology Foundation

**Focus:** STEM education, community support
**Eligible:** 501(c)(3) nonprofits in Micron communities (Boise area)
**Award Range:** Varies
**Deadline:** Ongoing
**Application:** Online portal
**Contact:** micron.com/foundation

## ELIGIBILITY ASSESSMENT WORKFLOW (S.05)

### Nonprofit Status Verification

**501(c)(3) vs Other Status:**
```
ELIGIBILITY HIERARCHY:

501(c)(3) Public Charity:
✓ Most foundation grants
✓ Most federal grants
✓ Most state grants
✓ Tax-deductible donations

501(c)(4) Social Welfare:
✓ Some federal grants
✓ Some state grants
✗ Most foundation grants
✗ Tax-deductible donations (limited)

501(c)(6) Trade Association:
✓ Very limited grants
✗ Most foundation grants
✗ Tax-deductible donations

Fiscal Sponsorship:
✓ Can apply under sponsor's 501(c)(3)
- Note in application
- Sponsor agreement required
```

**Verification Steps:**
1. Check IRS Tax Exempt Organization Search: apps.irs.gov/app/eos/
2. Confirm Idaho Secretary of State registration
3. Verify good standing (annual reports current)
4. Check nonprofit vs for-profit eligibility for specific grant
5. Confirm geographic service area matches grant requirements

---

## APPLICATION SUPPORT TOOLS (S.06)

### Budget Development Template

```
GRANT BUDGET TEMPLATE:

PERSONNEL:
- Executive Director (25% FTE) - $XX,XXX
- Program Manager (100% FTE) - $XX,XXX
- Program Staff (2 FTE) - $XX,XXX
- Fringe Benefits (30%) - $XX,XXX
Subtotal Personnel: $XX,XXX

OPERATING EXPENSES:
- Office Rent - $XX,XXX
- Utilities - $X,XXX
- Supplies - $X,XXX
- Equipment - $XX,XXX
- Travel (mileage, lodging) - $X,XXX
- Professional Services - $XX,XXX
- Insurance - $X,XXX
- Communications (phone, internet) - $X,XXX
Subtotal Operating: $XX,XXX

PROGRAM EXPENSES:
- Program supplies/materials - $XX,XXX
- Client assistance - $XX,XXX
- Training/workshops - $X,XXX
- Marketing/outreach - $X,XXX
Subtotal Program: $XX,XXX

INDIRECT COSTS:
- Indirect rate: X% of [direct costs / personnel]
- Calculation: $XXX,XXX × X% = $XX,XXX

TOTAL PROJECT COST: $XXX,XXX
Grant Request: $XX,XXX
Match/Cost Share: $XX,XXX
  - In-kind: $XX,XXX
  - Cash: $XX,XXX
```

### Logic Model Framework

```
LOGIC MODEL:

INPUTS → ACTIVITIES → OUTPUTS → OUTCOMES → IMPACT

INPUTS (Resources):
- Staff (FTE)
- Funding ($)
- Facilities
- Partners
- Materials/equipment

ACTIVITIES (What you do):
- [Specific programs/services]
- [Training provided]
- [Outreach conducted]

OUTPUTS (Direct products):
- # people served
- # workshops held
- # hours of service
- # materials distributed

SHORT-TERM OUTCOMES (1 year):
- Knowledge gained
- Skills developed
- Attitudes changed

MEDIUM-TERM OUTCOMES (2-3 years):
- Behavior change
- Condition improvement
- Status change

LONG-TERM IMPACT (3-5 years):
- Community-level change
- Systems change
- Sustained improvement
```

---

## COMPLIANCE TRACKING (S.08)

### Post-Award Compliance Checklist

```
GRANT COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST:

□ Grant Agreement signed and filed
□ Reporting schedule created (quarterly/annual)
□ Budget tracking system established
□ Separate accounting codes for grant funds
□ Match/cost-share tracking system
□ Required insurance obtained
□ Personnel hired/assigned
□ Procurement policies followed (if federal $)
□ Timeline/milestones documented
□ Partnership agreements executed
□ Evaluation plan implemented
□ Data collection system established

ONGOING REQUIREMENTS:
□ Financial reports submitted on time
□ Progress reports submitted on time
□ Budget modifications approved if needed
□ Site visits scheduled/completed
□ Audit requirements met (if applicable)
□ Match documentation maintained
□ Photos/success stories collected
□ Expenditures documented properly
□ Timesheets maintained (if salary charged)
□ Procurement documentation filed

CLOSEOUT REQUIREMENTS:
□ Final financial report
□ Final narrative report
□ Evaluation results
□ Sustainability plan
□ Unused funds returned
□ Equipment inventory (if applicable)
□ Lessons learned documented
```

**END OF IDAHO GRANT FINDER SLEEVE STRUCTURE**
